summary: Provision new FSx file systems: FSx for Windows File Server (SMB shares for Windows workloads) and FSx for Lustre (high-performance scratch/persistent storage). Greenfield today (no FSx file system exists yet in this estate) — each create stands up a complete file system; directory joins beyond AWS Managed AD, data repository associations, and backup/restore are engineer follow-ups.

operations.title: Provision a new FSx for Windows File Server
operations.summary: Request a brand-new SMB file share for Windows workloads; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up an FSx for Windows File Server as a complete specification: network placement, capacity, throughput, and directory integration. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: fs_name
params.label: File system name
params.help: Lower-case letters, numbers and dashes, like app-fileshare. Names the file system in the generated draft (FSx has no name attribute of its own — only the Name tag below).

params.name: subnet_ids
params.label: Subnets
params.help: One subnet for Single-AZ, two (different AZs) for Multi-AZ.

params.name: security_group_ids
params.label: Security groups
params.help: Who can reach the file system over SMB.

params.name: deployment_type
params.label: Deployment type
params.help: Multi-AZ adds a standby file server with automatic failover.

params.name: storage_capacity
params.label: Storage capacity (GiB)
params.help: SSD storage size, 32–65,536 GiB.

params.name: storage_type
params.label: Storage type
params.help: HDD is only valid for Single-AZ 2/Multi-AZ deployments and trades cost for latency.

params.name: throughput_capacity
params.label: Throughput capacity (MBps)
params.help: Sustained throughput per file server.

params.name: active_directory_id
params.label: AWS Managed Microsoft AD
params.help: The AWS Managed Microsoft AD this file system joins. Leave blank and an engineer sets up a self-managed AD join instead.

params.name: kms_key_id
params.label: Encryption key
params.help: Leave blank for the AWS-managed FSx key.

params.name: automatic_backup_retention_days
params.label: Backup retention (days)
params.help: 0 disables automatic daily backups.

params.name: weekly_maintenance_start_time
params.label: Weekly maintenance window
params.help: day:HH:MM in UTC, e.g. 3:02:00 for Wednesday 02:00 UTC. Leave blank for an AWS-assigned window.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What runs on this file share
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this file system.

decisions: AWS Managed Microsoft AD (active_directory_id) is the common case; a self-managed Active Directory join (self_managed_active_directory, including its service-account password) is an engineer follow-up.
DNS aliases, audit-log configuration, and disk IOPS provisioning are engineer follow-ups after creation.

operations.title: Provision a new FSx for Lustre file system
operations.summary: Request a brand-new high-performance scratch or persistent Lustre file system; an engineer reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up an FSx for Lustre file system as a complete specification: network placement, deployment type, and capacity. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: fs_name
params.label: File system name
params.help: Lower-case letters, numbers and dashes, like ml-training-scratch. Names the file system in the generated draft (FSx has no name attribute of its own — only the Name tag below).

params.name: subnet_ids
params.label: Subnets
params.help: A single subnet — Lustre file systems place their primary network interface in one Availability Zone.

params.name: security_group_ids
params.label: Security groups
params.help: Who can reach the file system over the Lustre client port.

params.name: deployment_type
params.label: Deployment type
params.help: SCRATCH is temporary, high-burst, and not replicated; PERSISTENT is long-lived and highly available.

params.name: storage_capacity
params.label: Storage capacity (GiB)
params.help: 1,200 GiB minimum; exact valid increments depend on the deployment type and are confirmed at review.

params.name: storage_type
params.label: Storage type
params.help: HDD is only valid for PERSISTENT_1.

params.name: data_compression_type
params.label: Data compression
params.help: LZ4 compresses data in flight and at rest at no extra cost for most workloads.

params.name: kms_key_id
params.label: Encryption key
params.help: Only used for PERSISTENT deployments; leave blank for the AWS-managed FSx key.

params.name: weekly_maintenance_start_time
params.label: Weekly maintenance window
params.help: day:HH:MM in UTC, e.g. 3:02:00 for Wednesday 02:00 UTC. Leave blank for an AWS-assigned window.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What workload uses this file system
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this file system.

decisions: per_unit_storage_throughput (required for PERSISTENT_1/PERSISTENT_2, invalid for SCRATCH types) is an engineer decision made at review time based on the chosen deployment type.
An S3 data repository association (import_path/export_path) and Multi-AZ subnet placement beyond the single subnet authored here are engineer follow-ups.
